quoteFirst: For the whole of that night did we continue beating up along the edge of the ice, in the teeth of a whole gale of wind; at last,
quoteTime: about nine o'clock in the morning
quoteLast: ,--but two short hours before the moment at which it had been agreed we should bear up, and abandon the attempt,--we came up with a long low point of ice, that had stretched further to the Westward than any we had yet doubled; and there, beyond, lay an open sea!
